commands.repair_reason: Needs contributor or maintainer live Mattermost proof before merge; I did not find a narrow code defect for an automated repair lane.; Cleared: The diff stays within Mattermost runtime/tests and does not touch workflows, dependencies, secrets, package metadata, or other supply-chain surfaces. commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Mattermost draft preview handling so block mode rotates preview posts at text/tool boundaries and the inbound off-preview path can honor explicit block streaming.

PR surface: Source +47, Tests +90. Total +137 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main unconditionally disables block streaming on the normal Mattermost inbound path and the draft stream edits the same post until its id is cleared. I did not run a live Mattermost server in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor or maintainer live Mattermost proof before merge; I did not find a narrow code defect for an automated repair lane.

target.title: fix(mattermost): preserve text-block boundaries in draft preview (#87322)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #87322 by giving Mattermost the same text-block boundary handling Discord has had for years. Today the draft preview edits a single post across every phase of a tool-heavy turn, so earlier text blocks disappear before the user reads them — Issue 1 in the report. The reporter's explicit `streaming: "off"` + `blockStreaming: true` config also failed because the inbound path forced `disableBlockStreaming: true` unconditionally — Issue 2 in the report.

This PR keeps the existing canonical `streaming.mode: "off" | "partial" | "block" | "progress"` contract on main (`src/config/types.base.ts:12`) — no schema change, no new config keys.

## Behavior change

`extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.ts` — inbound dispatch path (`monitorMattermostProvider`):

- **Block-reply path now honored when preview is off.** Replaced the hardcoded `disableBlockStreaming: true` with the same conditional already used by the button-click (`monitor.ts:832`) and slash-command (`monitor.ts:1047`) paths: when `draftPreviewEnabled` is true the preview is still the visible sink (no change), but when `streaming.mode === "off"` the path now honors the operator's explicit `account.blockStreaming` setting so block replies create separate posts via `createMattermostPost`.
- **Preview rotates at text-block boundaries when `streaming.mode === "block"`.** New `createMattermostDraftPreviewBoundaryController` in `draft-stream.ts` holds a "did we just stream something?" gate. At `onAssistantMessageStart`, `onReasoningEnd`, and `onToolStart` the controller calls `draftStream.forceNewMessage()` (the primitive already on the stream — `draft-stream.ts:133`) if and only if a draft update was emitted since the last boundary. The next `draftStream.update(...)` then runs the `else` arm of `sendOrEditStreamMessage` (`draft-stream.ts:92-104`) and `POST /posts` creates a new Mattermost post; earlier sealed posts stay visible. The gate is the same shape Discord uses (`extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.draft-preview.ts:132-138`) — back-to-back boundaries without content never emit empty preview posts.
- **All other modes unchanged.** `streaming.mode === "partial"` (default) keeps the edit-in-place feel. `"progress"` keeps its single rolling progress post. `"off"` keeps its no-preview behavior.

`extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/draft-stream.ts` — added `createMattermostDraftPreviewBoundaryController({ enabled, forceNewMessage })`. Local helper, no exposure through `api.ts`.

`extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/draft-stream.test.ts` — added unit tests for the controller (enabled/disabled, content gate, back-to-back boundaries) and for `draftStream.forceNewMessage()` itself (next update creates a new `POST /posts` call).

`extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.inbound-system-event.test.ts` — extended the existing `vi.mock("./draft-stream.js", …)` shim with a no-op `createMattermostDraftPreviewBoundaryController` stub so the suite keeps wiring through the mocked draft module.

## Diff size

```
 .../mattermost/src/mattermost/draft-stream.test.ts | 88 +++++++++++++++++++++-
 .../mattermost/src/mattermost/draft-stream.ts      | 26 +++++++
 .../monitor.inbound-system-event.test.ts           |  4 +
 extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.ts    | 25 +++++-
 4 files changed, 140 insertions(+), 3 deletions(-)
```

## Test plan

- [x] New regression test: `draftStream.forceNewMessage()` causes the next update to call `POST /posts` (not `PUT /posts/{id}`), so the next block becomes its own Mattermost post.
- [x] New regression test: `createMattermostDraftPreviewBoundaryController` skips `forceNewMessage` when no content streamed since the last boundary (no empty preview posts on back-to-back boundaries).
- [x] New regression test: controller never calls `forceNewMessage` when `enabled: false` (so `streaming.mode === "partial"` keeps edit-in-place).
- [x] Focused suite: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/draft-stream.test.ts` → 15/15 passed.
- [x] Focused suite: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.test.ts` → 56/56 passed.
- [x] Focused suite: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/accounts.test.ts` → 10/10 passed.
- [x] Focused suite: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.inbound-system-event.test.ts` → 8/8 passed.
- [x] Mattermost extension suite: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/mattermost/src/` → 40 files / 441/441 passed.
- [x] Typecheck: `pnpm tsgo:core`, `pnpm tsgo:extensions`, `pnpm check:test-types` → all exit 0.
- [x] Lint: `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/` → exit 0.
- [x] Format: `oxfmt --check` on all four touched files → all good.
- [x] Config metadata: `pnpm check:bundled-channel-config-metadata` → exit 0 (no schema change; canonical `streaming.mode` values already shipped).
- [ ] Live Mattermost server run with a tool-heavy turn under `streaming.mode: "block"` — see *What was not tested*.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Mattermost draft preview overwrites earlier text blocks within a single turn, and `streaming: "off" + blockStreaming: true` does not produce separate posts (#87322 Issue 1 and Issue 2).

**Real environment tested:** Node 26.0.0, vitest 4.1.7, pnpm 11.2.2, macOS 26.0 (Darwin 25.3.0) local checkout at `upstream/main@5d437de70f` + this PR's commit `d969eefa889ae5aa83a53a84376f406db0592922`. Tests exercise the production `createMattermostDraftStream` against a `MattermostClient` whose `request` impl is a `vi.fn()` recording every HTTP call, so the create-vs-edit distinction is checked at the same `POST /posts` / `PUT /posts/{id}` boundary the live Mattermost API exposes (`client.ts:492`).

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```
git checkout fix/87322-mattermost-block-preview-boundaries
pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/draft-stream.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/accounts.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.inbound-system-event.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/mattermost/src/
pnpm tsgo:core
pnpm tsgo:extensions
pnpm check:test-types
pnpm check:bundled-channel-config-metadata
```

**Evidence after fix:**

- `creates a new post on the next update after forceNewMessage` → asserts `calls.length === 2` and both calls hit `/posts` (not `/posts/post-1`); second post id is `post-2`. Same `MattermostClient` mock as the existing draft-stream tests (`createMockClient` at `draft-stream.test.ts:10`), so the create/edit branch is the production one in `sendOrEditStreamMessage` (`draft-stream.ts:88-104`).
- `calls forceNewMessage on boundary when enabled and content was streamed` → 1 call.
- `skips forceNewMessage when no content was streamed since the last boundary` → exactly 1 call across `noteBoundary, noteBoundary, noteUpdate, noteBoundary, noteBoundary`.
- `never calls forceNewMessage when disabled` → 0 calls across alternating `noteUpdate`/`noteBoundary`.
- Mattermost extension suite: 40 files / 441/441 tests passed after this change, including the inbound dispatch tests that load `monitor.ts` end-to-end (the mock for `./draft-stream.js` had to be extended with a `createMattermostDraftPreviewBoundaryController` no-op stub for the dispatch path to wire up).

**Observed result after fix:**

- With `streaming.mode === "block"`, each text/tool transition in the dispatch callbacks (`onAssistantMessageStart`, `onReasoningEnd`, `onToolStart`) seals the current preview post and the next draft update creates a new Mattermost post via `POST /posts`. The finalization step (`buildFinalEdit` at `monitor.ts:359-383`) still edits the *current* (last) preview post, so the final text only replaces the last preview block — earlier blocks stay as their own posts. This resolves Issue 2 in the report (finalization no longer wipes accumulated content because the accumulated content lives in earlier sealed posts).
- With `streaming.mode === "off"` and explicit `blockStreaming: true`, the inbound dispatch path no longer forces `disableBlockStreaming: true`, so block replies flow through the normal block-reply pipeline and create separate Mattermost posts via the SDK's block-reply path — matching Discord. This resolves Issue 1 in the report.
- With `streaming.mode === "partial"` (default) the boundary controller's `enabled` flag is `false`, so `forceNewMessage` is never called and the edit-in-place feel is preserved for users who want it.

**What was not tested:** A live Mattermost server with a tool-heavy turn under `streaming.mode: "block"` was not exercised in this PR — I do not have a self-hosted Mattermost 11.6.2 reachable from this checkout. The proof is source-backed (the same `createMattermostPost` / `updateMattermostPost` distinction the live API exposes, asserted at the recorded-request boundary), plus the existing 441-test Mattermost suite that loads `monitor.ts` end-to-end through the dispatch mocks. CLAWsweeper's own review of the related PR #75256 took the same source-level repro path and flagged it as "Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence path" — this PR uses the same evidence shape on the fix side.

## Refs

- Fixes #87322
- Related (not closed): #75256 — overlapping branch attempted a narrower `streaming.mode = "partial" | "block"` config that CLAWsweeper flagged as schema-narrowing. This PR keeps the canonical `streaming.mode` contract intact and only attaches per-mode behavior to the existing `"block"` value.
- Mirrors the Discord boundary pattern at `extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.draft-preview.ts:132-138`.

cc the reporter on #87322 for the live Mattermost verification path.
target.author: yetval
